prompt: I want to build a simple node editor in my React app using Rete.js. I need two types of nodes: a 'Number' node that has a numeric input control and one output port, and an 'Add' node that takes two number inputs and produces one output. I want users to be able to drag to connect nodes, and I need to be able to compute the result of the graph when a button is clicked. Show me the full setup.

assertions.text: Imports and uses DataflowEngine from rete-engine with editor.use(engine)
data() method on AddNode reads inputs with double-bracket array access pattern (e.g. inputs.left?.[0]?.[0]) rather than single-bracket (inputs.left?.[0])
ReactPlugin is initialized with { createRoot } argument (required for React 19)
Plugin wiring follows correct order: editor.use(area), area.use(connection), area.use(render)
engine.reset() is called before engine.fetch() to clear cached results
